sections.heading: 1 Introduction
sections.body:
This document describes the functionality of the advertising management system alfa AdSuite Enterprise (alfa ASE).

The document is outlined into two parts. Part 1 contains the commercial functionalities of the application. Part 2 describes all technical workflows and the functional scope of the software.

Both documents are based on the current software release (2.01.27). In case that extensions, which are planned for later software releases, are already specified in this document, these parts of the text are marked with a specific font face.

The following description specifies the functionality of the software, but doesn’t substitute a user training. The document may build the basis of a functional specification and me be useful in daily work as a compendium. The shown images may differ from those of the current software release.

A separate specification is available for the administration part of the alfa ASE.

sections.heading: 2 AdSuite Client
sections.body: The following chapter encloses all information about the ad management and purely technical applications. It is used as an overview and for the understanding of the applications functionalities. If necessary this documentation mentions parts of the technical application as well.

sections.heading: 2.1 Application Start
sections.body:
In general the alfa AdSuite Enterprise is built up and structured in a way, with a navigation area – comparable to the MS Windows Explorer on the left side of the window. alfa ASE uses tabs as user interface elements, which enables the user to switch between different tabs. With the help of the tabs it is possible to navigate within the master data of a customer, but also to change, e.g., between master and order data.

After the start of the application the central search dialogue for customers, orders and image data appears automatically (customizable).


Figure 1: Startup Window after Starting the AdSuite Application

In this window customer and order records can be selected. When a search result is accessed only the customer master data are displayed, if the dialogue is used in the customer mode. Opening an order record will force the customer data to appear automatically. This and further functions will be discussed in the following chapters.

sections.heading: 2.2 Menus
sections.body:
The top-most user interface element on the alfa AdSuite Enterprise screen contains the main menu with seven menu items.
Menu „File“

With the „Print“ function either a customer master data sheet or (in case, that an order is activated) an order data sheet can be printed (order data sheet not yet implemented).

The function “Exit” quits the alfa AdSuite Enterprise application.


Figure 2: Menu „File“

Menu: „Administration“

With this function the alfa AdSuite Enterprise administration module will get started. The functionality of this module will be explained in a separate documentation.


Figure 3: Menu „Administration“
Menu: „Contractor“

This menu enables to switch between different contractors (= companies). A customer always is opened in the context of the selected contractor. This contractor´s context defines all commercial and customizable data (e.g. price lists, credit limits, special discounts, invoicing parameter etc.), which may differ for the same customer depending on the contractor. This allows a legally independent company (e.g. as a member of a publishing house group) to share the customer´s address data while using different commercial parameters.

Figure 4: Menu „Contractor“
Menu: „Order“

The functions within this menu allows the input of new orders. Moreover the user can search for already saved orders.


Figure 5: Menu „Order“

Menu: „View“

This menu is only enabled when the order tab is active.

With the function “Maximize Work Area” the size of the editor window (PageOne XT) can be changed.

With the function “Show/Hide Navigation Tree” the navigation area (on the left side) can be shown or hidden.

With the function “Show/Hide Customer Data” the information about the customer data (upper part of the window) can be displayed or hidden.

The function “Page Measure” shows the currently valid page measurement (e.g. column width).





Figure 6: Menu „View“

The submenu „Table Setup“ is divided into several functions. The submenu “Modify” allows the user to add or delete several fields in the order data display.


Figure 7: Submenu „Table Setup“

„Show/Hide Table“ allows to toggle the display of the table.

With the function “Group Table” an additional table element becomes visible. This element allows the definition of additional constraints on the order data. For example, regarding an order consisting of many publication dates in several editions, the user is able to define a selection which shows only the publication dates within one specific edition.

The menu “Main Table” shows the order table without minimizing the space (like in “Show Table”).

The menu “Aggregation Table” shows an additional table row in which all numeric table fields are immediately summed up.

In combination with an active customer master data panel the menu “Address” (not yet implemented) is shown instead of the menu “Table Setup”.

Menu „Help“

The help function and the customizing information mode is not fully implemented yet. The function “Inform... [TRUNCATED]

sections.heading: 2.3 Customer and Order Search and Display
sections.body:
Regardless of the functionality, the next two following figures show the display of customer master data and order data as an example. Details of the functionality will be explained on the following pages.


Figure 11: Customer Master Data Display


Figure 12: Display of an Order of the opened Customer

sections.heading: 2.3.1 Search for Customer and Orders and Advertisements
sections.body:
The next icons describe the functionality of the customer and order search in detail. Usually the search dialogue pops up automatically after starting the application.

Explanation of the tool bar icons needed for the search functionality


In case the search dialogue is not displayed after the application startup or is needed at a later point in time, it can be called with these three icons in the tool bar. The left icon is needed for the customer search, the one in the middle for the order search and the right-most button for the search for advertisements.


Start search using the entered search criteria.


Reset all search criteria.


Close search dialogue.


Open one or multiple data records. A single record can be opened by a double click, too.


Figure 13: Search for Customers/Orders/Advertisements

The input fields in the upper area of the window are used to define a selection for a customer, order or ad list. It is not mandatory to type the name into the name field but it may be entered in any of the fields customer number, order code or invoice number, and vice versa. All available fields can be used to define the selection, to shorten the result list while searching for very common names e.g. Smith or Miller.

With a click on the item “Additional Filters” additional tab panes can be opened. The functionality of these dialogues will be explained on the following pages.

Whenever the radio button „Customer“ is selected, a list of customers matching the entered search criteria will be displayed. The next figure shows a query for the customer name “Miller“.


Figure 14: Search for Customer and Result Display (1 Customer)

In case that more than one customer with the same name exists, a list of all customers matching the search criteria will be displayed. Either the search may be further restricted or the requested customer record has already been found and  may be  picked from the list.
Figure 15: Search for Customers and Result Display (4 Customer with the same name)

The same possibilities exist for the order search. Opening an order record will automatically open the appropriate customer record.


Figure 16: Search for Orders and Result List (8 Orders)

When the radio button “Advertisements” is selected, only advertisements will be looked for. Due to the fact, that an order may consist of several advertisements (different ad images, publication dates etc.), the search for all advertisements of a specific publication date can result in a different hit count than the same query for all orders of the same date.


Figure 17: Search for Advertisements and Result Display (12 advertisements belonging to 8 orders)

The check box “As Payer” is an additional criterion for the order search concerning orders with a payment split for several customers. The query result for a specific customer name in combination with the radio button „Order“ and the check box „As Payer“ is a list of orders where this customer pays a partial amount of the order or the... [TRUNCATED]

sections.heading: 2.3.2 alfa ASE Explorer
sections.body:
After a customer or an a order has been selected from the data base, the navigation area on the left side of the application shows a structure pretty similar to the display of the MS Windows Explorer.


All in all four tab panes with different functionalities are displayed in the ASE Explorer tree view..

Selecting the tab pane “ASE” results in the display of customer master data and order data in the working area of the alfa ASE application.

The branch items shown underneath of „Customer Data“ corresponds to the tab panes within the working area. A mouse click on such an item does have the same effect as a click on the tab pane – it leads to the appropriate panel in the working area.

Figure 31: Explorer View of Customer Data


The tab pane „Structure“ shows the production structures of the commercial objects, editions, classes etc. customized with the alfa ASE administration application.

Each commercial object with its associated structures can be expanded and collapsed.



Figure 32: Explorer View of the Production Structure


The tab pane „CRM“ will be the entry point of the embedded Customer Relationship Management Functionality. This topic is not yet implemented.
Figure 33: Explorer View CRM



The tab pane „Statistics“ is the entry point for different turnover and amount statistics.

The statistics allow different selection criteria and allows to summarize turnover and amount per product, customer, order and customer criteria, sales representatives, edition and edition parts.



Figure 34: Explorer View of Statistics


The following figure shows a amount example for one specific edition in September 2005. The other statistics are build up in a similar way.


Figure 35:  Amount Statistic for the Month September

Statistics will  be described in detail in a specific chapter of this documentation.

sections.heading: 2.3.3 Customer and Order Display
sections.body:
With the possibilities for customer and order search described so far the user can select customers and orders from the database. If an order is opened, the associate customer data will be opened automatically.

The next figure once again shows the first panel of the customer master data. If a customer contract is assigned to the customer, the tab pane title “Customer Contract” will be displayed in red color.


Figure 36:  First Panel of Customer Master Data (Tab Pane Title “Customer Contract” in red emphasises that a contract is assigned to the customer)

The upper part of the customer working area summarizes the most important informations concerning this customer including the information about the responsible sales representative.


Figure 37: Customer Information
Inside the customer information, additional information e.g. about the customer type is displayed.

Furthermore the panel shows information about the current customer turnover (all existing orders in Fig. 39, already settled orders in Fig. 38). These values can be toggled using the “€” button. Both values can be constricted to a specific period of time in the current and the last year. Underneath these values the liable person is visible, which had been entered in the “Additional Customer Data”.



Figure 38: Already cleared Order Data

Figure 39: All Existing Order Data


Finally the date of the last order is displayed. With a click on this date the associated order will be opened and displayed.

Note: Some of the mentioned informations can be disabled depending on the user role.

sections.heading: 2.4 Customer Data
sections.body:
The creation of a new customer record takes place in the customer quick entry mask, where the most important customer data can be typed in. With the question mark symbol the user is able to call a separate query dialog for zip codes, streets and cities.


Figure 40: Customer Data Entry with using the Zip Code Search

The search for a zip code can be done with a phonetic search algorithm and can include street names or company names. A city or a street name will be found automatically, if the associated zip code has been typed in. It is not mandatory to search for the zip code in the separate zip code query window. The city will be found either in the customer quick entry mask or the tab pane “Adress”.

With the question mark symbol near the bank identification code the application jumps into a specific search dialogue for banks and their identification codes. If the bank identification code is entered directly the associated bank will be filled out automatically. For german bank account the application checks whether the bank account number is valid in combination with the bank identification code. A list of zip codes and bank identification codes is part of the alfa ASE database.


Figure 41: Customer Data Entry with using the Bank Identification Code Search

With the button “Switch to Detail Mode” for customer, the user can switch to the tab pane „Address“ in the customer data after finishing the input in the quick data entry mode. This allows the user to enter additional information to the several panels if necessary or needed.


Figure 42: Switch from Quick Data Entry Mode to Detail Mode for Customer Data

sections.heading: 2.5.1 Panel „Customer Master Data“
sections.body: The panel „Customer Master Data“ includes further 16 panel. The panel „Adress“ is initially activated.

sections.heading: 2.5.1.1 Panel „Address“
sections.body:
Figure 43: Display of the Customer Data with its valid Main Address
Several Addresses
It is possible to assign several addresses to one customer. In addition to the compulsive main address the user may define e.g. different invoice addresses, post box addresses, addresses for proof delivery etc. A validity date (from – to) may be attached to each address. In case of the main address alfa ASE  makes sure that there are no overlapping periods or periods without a valid main address.

The upper list shows the already defined addresses of a customer. The first row always contains the main address, all other addresses are sorted by descending validity date.

Address Type:
The address type defines different types of addresses like main address and additional addresses like invoicing address, post box address, address for proof delivery etc. The definition of an address type is mandatory.

Main Address:
At every point of time there is always exactly one main address. The validity date of the main address can not be modified through the user but is set automatically. The application automatically avoids periods with overlapping validity dates and periods without a valid main address.

Other Adresses:
It is possible to assign any count of additional addresses with different validity dates to a customer. The validity dates of the other addresses of the same type may either differ, overlap or include gaps.

The appropriate address will be used depending on the context. Without the definition of an additional address for a specific purpose the main address will get used.
Any wanted validity dates may be entered for the additional addresses.


Figure 44: A Customer with Main Address and Different Addresses as Invoicing Address and Post Box Address

sections.heading: 2.5.1.2 Panel “Communication”
sections.body:
In this panel the different communication ways to the customer can be defined. Phone numbers and email addresses can be split into private and business communication ways. alfa ASE provides a CTI (computer telephony integration) interface which routes incoming calls to the application. The caller is recognized automatically using the caller id (phone number). The user is notified of an incoming call by a small popup window, which offers the possibility to open the appropriate customer record directly. Outgoing calls can be initiated by clicking the icon beside the phone number.

Depending on the selected row in the communication list, the displayed information changes e.g. a click on phone number shows the international area code, the area code and the phone number. For each type of communication it is possible to define the preferred communication way.

During the entry or the modification of data in alfa ASE this button  transfers the information in the list box. Additional this button  saves the created or modified data into the database. This behaviour is similar in each dialogue of the  alfa ASE system. Within the administration application can be defined that at least one phone number is mandatory. Depending on the customizing in this case either a warning appears or the user is not able to save the record in the database.


Figure 45: Panel „Communication“ with Phone Number Information

Clicking on the email radio button enables the data entry for different email addresses.


Figure 46: Panel „Communication“ with Email Information

sections.heading: 2.5.1.3 Panel „Contacts“
sections.body:
This panel allows the user to enter any count of contacts for a customer (e.g. a company). The panel itself provides three further panels.

Panel „Contact ( Address“
Within the panel „Address“ the name and the address information can be entered (similar to the customer panel “Address”). The mandatory count of contacts for commercial customers is customizable within alfa ASE Administration. The way of handling a missing contact information (warning or error on saving the customer record) is definable in the customizing as well.  If the address of a contact is not known, the contact may be saved without address information as well.



Figure 47: Panel „Contact ( Address“
Panel „Contact ( Communication“
This panel behaves in identical way like the panel „Customer ( Communication“ described above.


Figure 48: Panel „Contact ( Communication“
Panel „Contact ( Criteria“

The funcitionality of this panel is identical to the panel „Customer ( Criteria“  mentioned later. The criteria displayed in the contact context can be defined within alfa ASE Administration.
Figure 49: Panel „Contact ( Criteria“ (only date of birth in this case)

sections.heading: 2.5.1.4 Panel „Documents“
sections.body:
This functionality allows the assignment of any type of document to the customer (or to a specific order).


Figure 50: Assignment of a Letter to a Customer (MS Word)

The application is not a restricted to specific document, but is able to assign any document format. Double clicking the document in the list opens the document assumed the generic application or a feasible viewer for the specific document format is available on the client (functionality is working on Windows PCs only).

sections.heading: 2.5.1.5 Panel „Business Lines“
sections.body:
Within this panel one or several business lines can be assigned to a customer. The business lines may build the basis for later reports. alfa ASE is delivered with a pre-defined business line directory. Publisher specific modifications of this directory can be done within alfa ASE Administration. The customizing as well allows the definition of a mandatory count of business lines for business customer and the behaviour of the application in case of disregarding this rule (warning or error).


Figure 51: Assignment of Business Lines to a Customer

sections.heading: 2.5.1.6 Panel „Criteria“
sections.body:
This panel allows the entry of publisher specific customer criteria. It is possible to define several types of criteria (numeric, alphanumeric, date) and associated user interface elements (popup menu with predefined lists, check boxes, text fields etc. The customizing can be done within alfa ASE Administration in a very simple way. Criteria can be customer dependent, contact dependent or order dependent.


Figure 52: Publisher Specific Criteria for Customers, Contacts and Orders

sections.heading: 2.5.1.7 Panel „Additional Data“
sections.body:
This panel is divided into six areas. The different functionality is described below:


Abbildung 53: Registerkarte Zusatzdaten mit sechs verschiedenen Merkmalen

Duplicates
The duplicate check, which runs as automatic server process, figures out which customer data records possibly are duplicates. These records are marked as “Duplicate Candidates” in this panel. Because alfa ASE is not able to make the final decision, which customer might be a duplicate, this decision has to be done by the user, by setting the checkmark to “Duplicate”. In this case it is necessary to define the original data record by using the customer search dialogue. Checking the check mark “Ignore Duplicate Check” would force the system to ignore this specific customer record during the duplicate check (normally because the customer record has been created intentionally). A duplicate check takes place as well during the data entry of a new customer. In case that the system detects a customer record to be a potential duplicate, the user receives a warning.

Confirmations
In the confirmations area it is possible to define the number and the type of proof delivered to the customer. Moreover it is definable, whether and on which communication way the customer gets a order confirmation. Concerning the order proofs the type (complete newspaper proof, single page proof, clipping proof, proof on invoice form) count and proof recipient (orderer and/or additional proof recipients) are definable.

Properties

In this panel several customer properties can be set, e.g. Local Customer, Permanent Customer, Customer Type (Unknown, Company, Person, Church, Political Party etc.), Liable Person.

A setting within alfa ASE Administration controls whether the declaration of a liable person for a company is mandatory or not. Additional the assignment of a VAT rate is necessary for customers. The radio button “Linked Company” is only enabled for the customer type “Company”, otherwise this button will be disabled.

A Debitor ID will be assigned automatically only for permanent customers.

Figure 54: Customer Properties


Credit Limit
Credit Limits and the stoppage of credits can be set in this panel. Assuming an existing interface to the financial accounting system the credit limit can be transferred via this interface. The stoppage of credits can be defined as prepayment or as complete stoppage. For the time being the information is only displayed.

The possibilities concerning the credit limits are depending on the used financial accounting system:

1. Open items will be fetched online from the financial accounting system while opening the customer record in alfa ASE. Therefore the timeliness is not dependent on the frequency of invoicing batch runs, but on the timeliness of the financial accounting data. Even open items of a customer which not have been charged by alfa ASE will be considered. If the sum of the open items increases the customers’ credit limit, normally no further advertisement or... [TRUNCATED]

sections.heading: 2.5.1.8 Panel „Discounts“
sections.body:
Within this panel different customer specific discounts for specific ad types, products, editions and classes can be defined. These discounts are customer contract independent. The discounts may be separately defined for ads and supplements. If the check box „Ads“ is checked, the discount can be limited to specific ad types, checking the „Supplement“ check box disables the ad types. Every discount is valid for a determined period of time.

The figure below shows a discount of two percent for every order published in  the product „A-NEWS”, the edition „WEST “ and the class “5110 (Austin)”.


Figure 56: Customer specific Discount (Customer Contract independent)

sections.heading: 2.5.1.9 Panel „Sales Reps“
sections.body:
alfa ASE knows two types of Sales Representative: A Sales Rep (as single person) and a Sales Rep Pool. Both share the following properties:

· a period of time, in which commissions should be paid (the reference date is the publishing date).

· entry date which means the company entry date for employed sales reps, contract start date for freelancer or creation date for a sales rep team.

The assignment of a sales rep to an ad or a supplement under specific circumstances on a specific publishing date can be done automatically or overruled by the user.

Within the panel „Sales Rep“ the sales rep data can be entered and the sales rep (team) can be assigned to an order or a customer. The panel in the customer master data shows the sales reps (team) who are assigned to this customer. The + sign starts a database query for sales reps and allows the user to assign a sales rep to the customer.


Figure 57: Assignment of a Sales Rep to a Customer

Moreover it is possible to define a commission split. This functionality only works in the commission mode “Split”. After selecting this mode the share field will become enabled and the required percentage may be entered. As additional information a commission scale and the duration of this commission contract can be entered.


Figure 58: Commission Parameters
Assigning Sales Representative to the Customer
The window „Search for Sales Reps“ opened by clicking on the „+“-sign is rather similar to the customer search. The major differences are:

Instead of customers and/or orders the user can only look for sales represantives (either single persons or teams).

Figure 59: This Dialogue allows the Assignment of Sales Reps (Teams) to a Customer

The both checkboxes „Team Members“ and „Customer Contract“ may be toggled. If none of the is selected, the meaning is “All Sales Reps” and “No Customer Contracts“.


Figure 60: Searching for all Sales Reps who are assigned to a Sales Rep Team (by checking the Check Box „Team Member, which is displayed in bold font face)

Figure 61: Searching for all Sales Reps who are not assigned to a Sales Rep Team (by unchecking the Check Box „Team Member“, font face remains bold)


Figure 62: Searching for all Sales Reps who are not assigned to a Customer Contract but not to a Sales Rep Team (by unchecking the Check Box „Team Member“, font face remains bold and checking the Check Box “Customer Contract” in bold font face)

After a successful query in the database the sales reps can be selected from the hit list and assigned to the customer. The assignment of one or several specific sales rep(s)  (teams) and the valid conditions for the publishing date of an advertisement or a supplement may be either taken from the customer master data or modified by the user. This assignment is the prerequisite for the payment of a sales commission. The assignment of sales rep to a customer is only possible within the handling of the customer master data (panel “Sales Reps”).

The same sales rep search d... [TRUNCATED]

sections.heading: 2.5.1.10 Panel „Customer Contracts“
2.5.1.11 General Information concerning Customer Contracts
sections.body:
Evaluation of a Customer Contract

Contract settlements can be executed as intermediate settlements for running contract sequences and as final settlements at the end date of a contract sequence. For these settlements the customer contract has to be evaluated.

Moreover a customer contract can be evaluated at any time to get the information about the current contract standings. The evaluation process takes place in several phases which are described below. Some of the phases refer on appearances, some on the complete contract sequence and some phases refer on both.

Phase I: Evaluation of Primary Amounts
First the value of the primary amounts has to be calculated considering the following rules and exceptions.

The following counting rules are valid for a specific appearance of an order (for the split part in case of a payment split) and defines, whether the appearance has to be completely counted for the contract sequence:

· the contractor or the company group member (in case of company group contracts) has to be identical:

· with the customer for customer contracts, with split part payer for the ad (in case of an order with a payment split). This is also valid with a customer contract with a defined agency.

· with the order agency for agency contracts.

· the appearance date has to be within the contract sequence duration.

· the counting unit (not the invoicing unit) has to be identical with the primary unit of the contract and the primary scale of the contract sequence. Moreover the counting unit has to be compatible with the contract class (ad contracts for ads, supplement contracts for supplements or turnover for both).Die Zähleinheit der Erscheinung (nicht die Berechnungseinheit) muss mit der primären Zähleinheit des Abschlusses und der primären Staffel der Abschlussfolge übereinstimmen und kompatibel zur Abschlussklasse sein (Anzeigen und Beilagen jeweils für sich, Umsatz für alle).

· Consideration of possibly existing counting rules (see above);

· Consideration of possibly existing exceptions on counting rules for specific ads (see above);

Aditionally the amount counting can be modified for each specific ad as follows:

· if column millimeter ads for pages or page parts occur with a different column count (e.g. five columns in the advertising part, six column in the text part of the newspaper), the amount is recalculated regarding the different column models. (For example 100 mm in a page part with five columns are counted as 120 mm, if the default column model of the contract is based on six columns.)

· If combinations are defined in a way that the appearance should only count once, than the specific appearance are only counted fractionally (e.g. ½ for a combination of two editions).

· For orders with a payment split only the split part of the payer has to be counted

These (eventually modified) amounts will be summed up, the results can be possibly modified as described below:

· if the contract sequence with a duration time of... [TRUNCATED]

sections.heading: 2.5.1.12 Panel „Master Data – Turnover Statistics“
sections.body:
In this panel turnover statistics can be executed with several pre-defined selection criteria. In opposite to the statistic functionality in the AdSuite Enterprise explorer (the navigation area), which allows statistics using all orders for specific editions, classes, sales reps etc. the customer statistic refers to the currently opened customer. The different selection criteria are displayed in the figure below.

Figure 98: Example of a Query for a specific Period of Time and Ad Type

Additional statistic about turnover and ad amounts can be executed in the AdSuite Enterprise explorer and are described in the appropriate chapter.

sections.heading: 2.5.1.13 Panel „Master Data – Agencies“
sections.body:
In this panel the assignment of an agency to a customer – or vice versa – can be done. Depending on the customer type the „+“ button opens the search dialogue for customers or agencies. When the search is started within a customer data record, only agencies can be selected.


Figure 99: Agency Panel after Assignment of a Customer to a Agency

In this panel the amount of the agency commission can be defined and assigned to specific conditions e.g. the order type (ads or supplements), the ad type (for ad orders only), specific commercial objects, editions and edition parts (classes, placements and advertorials). The following figure shows an example, where the agency gets a commission of 3.5% for all orders of the customer „Schäfer“, which are lineage ads (no other ad types or supplements) apperaring in the edition “WEST” of the commercial object “A-NEWS”.


Figure 100: Definition of Commission Percentage and Conditions

The agency customers may appear explicitly as customers or remain anonymous. The relationship between the agency and the customer can be defined by the user or can be created automatically, whenever an agency books an order for a customer. The conditions mentioned before can only be assigned to the agency itself not to the relationship between agencies and customers.

Customers can order ads or other achievements directly or via an agency. Even if a customer books an order via an agency his master data can be used to evaluate customer specific values (e.g. customer contract discounts).

sections.heading: 2.5.1.14 Panel „Master Data - Payment Information“
sections.body:
In this panel different payment informations like bank account and credit card informations can be entered. For each bank account it is definable whether the customer allows a direct debit. If more than one bank or credit card accounts are defined, it is possible to define a preferred account for each payment type.

The credit card informations have only information purposes yet. There is no interface connected to a cashier terminal or an credit card clearing organisation.

For every customer data record several bank account records can be defined and used in selectable contexts. This information are used as default for each new order.


Figure 101: Payment Informations

An embedded bank identification code (BIC) search can be used while entering a bank identification code. The question mark sign opens a specific BIC query window.


Figure 102: Search for Bank or Bank Identification Code (BIC)

sections.heading: 2.5.1.15 Panel „Master Data – Invoices“
sections.body:
This panel automatically lists all invoices created for this customer. The list may be restricted to specific invoice dates or payment methods.


Figure 103: List of all Invoices


The buttons in the bottom left corner activates the different functions:

Tool Symbol

Opens an Invoice
Preview

AdSuite Enterprise Page Preview
PDF Preview

Page Preview as PDF  File
Printer


Prints the current Invoice

If an invoice has to modified, it can be opened with the tool symbol. The red cross symbol in the invoice header allows the cancellation of the whole invoice with all invoice positions. The user have to confirm a prompt, whether the invoice should really be completely cancelled.

In the invoice position area single invoice positions can be cancelled directly. Hint: Instead of changing existing invoices a modification uses the original invoice positions as intial point to modify the appropriate invoicing status or the value of an order position. The appropriate invoices and credit invoices are created by the invoicing batch process.

An invoice modification is especially necessary for the handling of customer complaints. The modification may lead to different actions e.g. the credit of a specific order position or the complete cancellation of an invoice with the creation of a new calculated invoice.

AdSuite doesn’t include a complaint handling functionality yet. This functionality can be realised with a CRM software or with the use of different additional order criteria.

The effect of the cancellation of a position or the complete orders on the customer contract settlement and/or sales rep provision can be defined for each contractor and process.

After the correction of the associate order a new invoice with a new invoice number will be created through the invoice batch process. The new invoice refers to the cancelled invoice.


Figure 104: List of invoiced Ads with various Modification Options

The tool symbol in the order position level allows to access the appropriate order  directly to perform modifications.


Figure 105: Direct Order Access

A page preview, the creation of a PDF file or  a printout of the invoice can be caused by the different print buttons. If the proof type option “Proof” is activated for the current customer, the image of the ad will be printed on the invoice. Following an example of an invoice (can be customized using XSLT-FO technology) is shown:





Figure 106: Invoice with Proof Image (Page 1: Image; Page 2: Proof)

sections.heading: 2.5.1.16 General Information concerning Invoicing
2.5.1.17 Panel „Master Data – Open Items”
sections.body:
The panel „Open Items“ is only available, if an financial accounting system (e.g. SAP FI, Varial etc.) managing these open items is interfaced to alfa AdSuite Enterprise. In the figure shown below the financial accounting system “Varial” is connected to alfa AdSuite Enterprise. The open item are queried directly from this system.


Figure 107: Open Items of a Customer

sections.heading: 2.5.1.18 Panel „Master Data – Invoice Parameters“
sections.body:
Within this panel customer specific information concerning the invoicing can be entered. These attributes describe the number of invoices and invoice copies, the invoicing period, the delivery type, the parameter for an optional invoice operation and the prefered payment method.


Figure 108: Invoice Parameters

sections.heading: 2.6.1.1 Panel „Ad Appearance“ (to be translated)
sections.body:
Nach Aufruf eines Auftrages ist automatisch die Registerkarte „Erscheinung“ selektiert. Dort stehen Informationen zu dem Auftrag, die bereits unter Pkt. 2.3.3 und den Folgepunkten erläutert sind.


Abbildung 77: Darstellung einer Anzeige im PageOne-Fenster mit den entsprechenden Registerkarten

sections.heading: 2.6.1.2 Panel „Prices“ (to be translated)
sections.body:
Neben der Preisinformation oben rechts, die unabhängig von einer Registerkarte immer gezeigt wird, gibt es eine gesonderte Registerkarte mit mehr Informationen zur Preisfindung und der Möglichkeit eine Modifikation des Preises vorzunehmen, z. B. Berechnung für Farbzuschläge etc. Die rechts unten dargestellten Informationen wie z. B. Chiffre sind noch nicht aktiviert. Mit einem Klick auf das Feld kann dieser Bereich aktiviert werden. Anschließend wird das Feld links in den Rabatten und Zuschlägen dargestellt und kann mit dem jeweiligen Preis versehen werden. Mit dem Button ganz unten rechts wird der Gesamtpreis neu berechnet.


Abbildung 78: Weitere Informationen zur Preisfindung sowie der Möglichkeit Änderungen vorzunehmen

sections.heading: 2.6.1.3.1 Terminology
sections.body:
Rates

Rates define a calculation unit and group prices using different logical criteria (e.g. “Company rate for black and white display ads using specific design formats”).

Prices

Prices are separated in regular and local prices. They can be valid on specific week days for a selected combination of editions and edition parts (classes). An example may be A “Regular Price for the car market of the edition CITY on Mondays and Thursdays”.

Price Lists

Price List determine the currency for all included prices and groups the prices within a given period of time. Price lists can be copied with the use of specific modification factors. Example: “Price List No. 2, valid for the first quarter of 2006”.

Price Modifier

Price Modifier changes a specific price under defined circumstances by recalculating it considering a given numeric factor. Examples might be “Double price for color ads” or „Half price for ads of political parties“.

Discount Schemes

Discount Schemes control the sequence and validity of percentual of absolute discounts and surcharges (e.g. customer specific discounts, agency commission, customer contract discount etc.). It is possible to define customer specific discount schemes.

Discount Restrictions

Discount Restrictions handle the validity of specific discounts and surcharges for specific ads (e.g. Customer specific discounts are not allowed for ads in the class „Family“ of the edition „OST“.

Discount Exceptions

Discount Exceptions defines dependencies between different discount types (e.g. agency provision is not granted for ads with customer specific discounts).

sections.heading: 2.6.1.3.2 Ad Price Calculation
sections.body:
Overview

1. Evaluation of valid rates

2. Definition of proper rate

3. Evaluation of base price before applying of price modifiers (BPbPM)
4. Applying of all matching price modifiers, results in the base price (BP)
5. Applying of all appropriate discounts and surcharges, results in the net price

6. Calculation and addition of the VAT results in the gross price.
Evaluation of Valid Rates

Rates matching the following conditions are selected from all available rates:

1. The appearance date of the ad is within the rates’ validity period.

2. The ordered amount of an achievement amount in terms of given calculation units (column millimetres, lines, words, pieces)  meets the limits (minimum amount – maximum amount) within the rate definition.

3. If the rate is restricted to a specific ad type (e.g. lineage ads, display ads), the ad has to be of this type.
4. If the rate is restricted to specific customer type (companies, private persons,  political parties etc.) the orderer has to be of this type.

5. If the rate is only valid for ads using specific price relevant group of sample formats, the ad has to use a sample format of this group.
6. The rate has to contain a price which is applicable to the conditions described below:
a. The appearance date of the ad is within the validity period of the price.
b. Edition and class assigned to the ad are valid for this price, considering the inheritance mechanism within the class hierarchy.

c. The price is valid for the weekday of the appearance date of the ad.

Definition of Proper Rate
If the result of the rate evaluation (described above) leads to more than one possible rate, the most specific rate will be selected. The validity criteria are ranked as follows:

a. Ad Type
b. Sample Format Group
c. Color Count
d. Customer Type
Evaluation of Base Price before Applying of Price Modifiers (BPbPM)

1. Selection of regular price or local price due to the order data or customer master data (the default value is defined in the customer master data and may be overwritten in the order).

2. p1 := Price per Unit  *  Amount of Units
3. p2 := Base Surcharge + p1
If p2 < Minimum Price
Then BPbPM := Minimum Price
Else


BPbPM:= p2
Applying of all matching Price Modifiers

Price Modifiers are assigned to a rate. They are positive factors which can be applied depending on following attributes:
a. Customer Type (Public Authorities, Private Person, Agency, Company, Church, Club or Political Party)
b. Sample Format Group (e.g. for family ads, obituaries etc.)
c. Colour Count
d. Ad Taking Deadline (regular deadline, express deadline);
e. Newspaper Part (Text Part, Ad Part);

f. Ad Type (Lineage Ad, Display Ad, DTP,…)

Additional Prerequisites:

· The price modifier belongs to the same price list as the price found;
· The appearance date of the ad is within the validity period of the price modifier.


May  PM1 to PMn the found applicable price modifiers. The base price will be calculated as follows:


BP :=
BPbPM  *  PM1  * ... [TRUNCATED]

sections.heading: 2.6.1.4 Panel „Production Details“ (to be translated)
sections.body:
Über diese Registerkarte werden die Motive zu den Erscheinungstagen gezeigt und können bearbeitet werden. Neue Motive können über die Funktionalitäten in dieser Registerkarte ebenfalls angelegt werden. Insbesondere wird darauf hingewiesen, ob das aktuell gewählte Motiv in einer Erscheinung verwendet wird, für die der Annahmeschluss bereits verstrichen ist. Sollte das der Fall sein, kann das Motiv nicht mehr geändert werden. Für die Gestaltung vom Fließsatz bis hin zur komplexen gestalteten Anzeige ist PageOne in der Lage durch hinterlegte Formate eine automatisierte Produktion zu ermöglichen.


Abbildung 79: Registerkarte Motiv mit Informationen zu den Motiven

Die Buttons auf der linken Seite der Registerkarte bieten verschiedene Möglichkeiten an, die nachfolgend erläutert werden:



Erstellung eines neuen Motivs


Bearbeiten eines vorhandenen Motivs (nur möglich bei zukünftigen
Erscheinungen)


Geht nicht – Was macht das ?????????????


Geht nicht – Was macht das ?????????????


Geht nicht – Was macht das ?????????????


Diese Funktionen sind bereits beschrieben worden. Sie stehen in
verschiedensten Bereichen zur Verfügung, die Funktionalität ist dabei überall identisch.

Falls mehrere Motive vorhanden sind oder neue hinzukommen, werden diese Motive mit einer Kennung von A bis Z versehen. Im rechten Bereich können für neue Motive verschiedene Vorgaben getätigt werden. Für das Motiv kann ein Editor ausgewählt werden, z. B. PageOne, Xpress bzw. Indesign. Der Anzeigentyp, die Vorlagen-Gruppe ??? sowie das Format der Anzeige kann bestimmt werden. Weitere Daten für die Höhe und Breite des Motivs sowie der Farbgebung können ebenfalls definiert werden.
Bei der Erstellung neuer Motive ist AdSuite in der Lage mit sog. Format-Galerien zu arbeiten. Dort können beliebige Muster hinterlegt werden, die in Wysiwyg angezeigt werden und durch Klick auf ein Muster in den Auftrag übernommen werden.


Abbildung 80: Format-Galerie mit Fließsatz-Mustern


Dabei kann sich bei den Format-Galerien auch um Gestaltete Anzeigen handeln wie im nächsten Bild zu erkennen ist.


Abbildung 81: Format-Galerie mit gestalteten Mustern


Die Möglichkeit zur Einrichtung einer Textbibliothek für wiederkehrende Texte ist ebenso möglich. Der Mustertext wird hier definiert und abgerufen.


Abbildung 113: Format-Galerie mit gestalteten Mustern

sections.heading: 2.6.1.5 Panel „Ad Box“ (to be translated)
sections.body:
In dieser Registerkarte können Daten für die Chiffreverwaltung gesichtet, verändert bzw. neu eingegeben werden. Dabei ist das Motiv immer als Wysiwyg-Darstellung zu erkennen. Falls eine Chiffre-Adresse hinterlegt wurde, ist diese im rechten Bereich sichtbar. Chiffre-Adressen sowie der Chiffre-Typ können pro Motiv hinterlegt werden.

Es kann definiert werden, ob es sich generell um einen Chiffre-Auftrag handelt, ob eine Zusendung oder Abholung gewünscht wird bzw. es sich um eine Tele-, E-Mail- oder Web-Chiffre handelt. Weitere Zusatzinformationen wie die zugehörigen Möglichkeiten der Kommunikationswege zum Kunden wie Telefon, E-Mail oder die URL der Web-Adresse können an dieser Stelle hinterlegt werden.


Abbildung 83: Registerkarte Chiffre mit den Motiven des Auftrags

sections.heading: 2.6.1.6 Panel „Order Data“ (to be translated)
sections.body:
Im linken Bereich der Auftragsdaten ist die Auftragsnummer abzulesen. Falls es eine Kunden-Auftrags-Nr. gibt, kann diese hier eingetragen werden. Der aktuelle Auftrags-Status wird angezeigt, kann aber nicht verändert werden. Unter dem Auftragsstatus wird der aktuelle Inserent angezeigt. Mit der Formulierung „Gebucht als“ kann definiert werden, ob der Inserent ein Ortskunde ist. Außerdem kann über eine Pop-Up-Auswahl die Definition des Kundentyps ausgewählt bzw. geändert werden.

Bei einem Auftrag besteht die Möglichkeit, den aktuellen Inserenten durch einen anderen zu ersetzen. Diese Möglichkeit wird durch den Button „Kunde auswählen“ bereit gestellt. Es öffnet sich das bereits bekannte Fenster für die Kundensuche, in dem ein Kunde gesucht und dem Auftrag zugeordnet werden kann.

Im rechten Bereich kann eine Auftragsbestätigung erzeugt werden, die entweder sofort gedruckt, als PDF-Datei gespeichert oder per Mail an eine beliebige Adresse versendet werden kann. Wie bei allen Funktionen des Druckens oder der PDF-Erzeugung in alfa AdSuite Enterprise stellt das Programm die notwendigen Daten per XML zur Verfügung. Per XSLT kann der Kunde die Gestaltung für seine Formulare selbst bestimmen und durchführen.


Abbildung 84: Registerkarte Chiffre mit den Motiven des Auftrags

sections.heading: 2.6.1.7 Panel „Supplements“ (to be translated)
sections.body:
Über diesen Reiter werden Beilagen erfasst. Anhand des Objektes, der Ausgabe, der Erscheinung sowie weiteren Eingabemöglichkeiten von Gewicht, Anzahl etc. können Beilagen den entsprechend definierten Strukturen zugeordnet werden.

Über die Administration wird die Beilagenbelegungshierarchie hinterlegt, die die Grundsubstanz der Beilagenverwaltung darstellt.


Abbildung 85: Registerkarte Fremdbeilage

sections.heading: 2.6.1.8 Panel „Documents“ (to be translated)
sections.body:
Auch diese Registerkarte existiert bereits bei den Stammdaten. Allerdings gilt auch hier, dass die zum Auftrag angehängten Dokumente unterschiedlich zu den Stammdaten sein können. Es kann durchaus sein, dass es zu einem Auftrag spezielle Dokumentationen geben muss.

Das Dokumentenformat spielt dabei keine Rolle. Es können MS Word, MS Excel oder andere    Dateitypen wie MPG-Videos etc. sein. Die Daten können direkt über diese Registerkarte an den Auftrag angehängt werden. Bei einem Doppelklick auf die entsprechenden Dateien in der Dokumentenliste öffnet sich das jeweilige Dokument in dem Programm, in dem es erstellt wurde, vorausgesetzt das jeweilige Programm ist auf dem Client vorhanden (gilt nur für Windows-PCs).


Abbildung 86: Registerkarte Dokumente mit zwei dem Auftrag zugeordneten Dokumenten

sections.heading: 2.6.1.9 Panel „Proofs“ (to be translated)
sections.body:
Über diese Registerkarte wird der Belegversand gesteuert. Dem Auftrag kann eine Belegadresse bzw. mehrere Adressen mitgegeben werden. Weiterhin kann definiert werden, welche Art von Beleg versendet werden soll (Vollbeleg, Ausschnittsbelege etc.).


Abbildung 87: Registerkarte Belege mit der Auswahl zwei verschiedener Belegadressen

sections.heading: 2.6.1.10 Panel „Payment“ (to be translated)
sections.body:
Diese Registerkarte enthält die Bankverbindungen sowie die aktuelle Rechnungsadresse des Kunden, die im Bereich der kommerziellen Eingabe (Registerkarte Adressen und Zahlungswege) hinterlegt wurden. Die Bankverbindungen des Kunden werden, falls mehrere vorhanden sind, angezeigt. Eine Zuordnung ist allerdings an dieser Stelle nicht möglich, da bei der kommerziellen Eingabe eine bestimmte Bankverbindung als „bevorzugt“ gekennzeichnet wurde, diese wird automatisch herangezogen.

Dem Auftrag kann eine bestimmte Rechnungsadresse zugeordnet werden. Die aktuelle Rechnungsadresse für den Auftrag ergibt sich wie folgt: aktuelle Rechnungsadresse = auftragsspezifische Rechnungsadresse, falls vorhanden und zeitlich gültig, andernfalls erstbeste zeitlich gültige Rechnungsadresse des Kunden. Falls nicht vorhanden, Hauptadresse des Kunden. In diesem Beispiel sind zwei Rechnungsadressen vorhanden, aus denen der Benutzer für diesen Auftrag gezielt eine Adresse auswählen kann. Die aktuell gewählte wird oben rechts angezeigt.

Möglich ist auch eine vom Normalfall abweichende Faktura für diesen Auftrag.


Abbildung 88: Registerkarte Zahlung mit den Bankverbindungen und der Rechnungsadresse des Kunden

sections.heading: 2.6.1.12 Panel „Sales Representatives”  (to be translated)
sections.body:
Der Reiter Vertreter ist bei den Stammdaten und beim Auftrag vorhanden, muss aber nicht in jedem Fall identisch sein. So kann ein Vertreter dem Kunden generell zugeordnet sein, aber für einen speziellen Auftrag kann durchaus auch ein anderer Vertreter zugeordnet werden.


Über das Plus-Zeichen lässt sich ein weiteres Fenster öffnen, in dem Vertreter gesucht und dem Auftrag zugeordnet werden können.

sections.heading: 2.6.1.13 Panel „Order Criteria“ (to be translated)
sections.body: Über den Reiter Kriterien können dem Auftrag weitere Informationen wie „in welcher Form liegt das Manuskript vor“, ob ein Motiv angeliefert wird, ob eine Satzarbeit notwendig ist oder ob Repros produziert werden müssen, hinterlegt werden. Die Kriterien können über die Administration frei konfiguriert werden.

sections.heading: 2.6.1.14 Panel „Memo“ (to be translated)
sections.body: Im Reiter Notizen können zum Auftrag beliebige Notizen hinterlegt werden.

sections.heading: 2.6.1.16 Panel „Contact Persons“ (to be translated)
sections.body:
Der Reiter Ansprechpartner ist z. Zt. beim Stammsatz und beim Auftrag identisch. Zukünftig wird es möglich sein, aus den beim Kunden hinterlegten Ansprechpartnern einen oder mehrere für einen Auftrag auszuwählen.












































































































































































































































































































































































































alfa Media Partner GmbH
Page 94
